mapping_title: Vendor report treated as compliance status
domain: Vendor risk, GRC, and compliance evidence assembly

native_domain_object.description: A vendor risk report containing observations, attestations, questionnaire answers, control descriptions, or review notes for a vendor or service provider.

source_claim.statement: The vendor report exists and contains the preserved statements, attachments, or observations recorded in the source context.
source_claim.scope: Report contents, issuer, date, referenced evidence, review context, and stated limitations only.

evidence_preserved.description: The vendor report reference is preserved as evidence that the report exists and was available to the consuming process.
Supporting attachments or questionnaire responses are preserved as referenced evidence.

boundary_crossing.transition_description: A vendor report is consumed by a compliance or GRC register as evidence relevant to compliance status, control posture, or residual risk.

downstream_assumption.statement: The existence or review of the vendor report is treated as if it establishes compliance status for the vendor, system, or control area.

unresolved_questions: What policy, control framework, or regulatory basis would authorize a compliance determination?
Which claims in the vendor report are independently verified, and which are self-attested?
What time period, system boundary, jurisdiction, and control scope would apply to any compliance claim?
non_claims: This mapping does not establish compliance.
This mapping does not establish audit acceptance.
This mapping does not establish vendor trustworthiness.
This mapping does not establish legal sufficiency.
This mapping does not convert a report into an approval.
